content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 1, 1988

Honorable David G. Kelley
Assemblyman, Seventy-Third District
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-073

Dear Assemblyman Kelley:

You have requested advice under the conflict of interest
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

Are numismatic coins held by one of your businesses as an
investment reportable on your statement of economic interests?

CONCLUSION

An investment in numismatic coins is not an "investment" for
reporting purposes under the Political Reform Act and, therefore, you
are not required to report it on your statement of economic interests.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires that elected officials file periodic
disclosure reports and report, among other things, "investments."
(Sections 87200-87210.) The Act defines "investment" as "... any
financial interest in a business entity...." (Section 82034, emphasis
added.)

A "business entity" is "...any organization or enterprise
operated for profit, including but not limited to a proprietorship,
partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture, syndicate,
corporation or association." (Section 82005.)

_________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Honorable David G. Kelley
March 1, 1988
Page Two

Because your ownership of numismatic coins does not meet the
Act's definition of "investment," you are not required to disclose
your interest in the coins.

If you have any questions about this advice, please call me
at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE
SACRAMENTO OFFICE:                                                    DISTRICT OFFICES:

☐ STATE CAPITOL                                                       ☐ 6840 INDIANA AVENUE
  P.O. BOX 942849                                                       SUITE 150
  SACRAMENTO 94249-0001                                                 RIVERSIDE 92506
  (916) 445-7852                                                        (714) 369-6644

  NANCY LUCCHESI                                                      MARGI WEGGLELAND
  SENIOR ASSISTANT                                                    ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT

COMMITTEES:                                                           ☐ 777 E. TAHQUITZ WAY
                                                                        SUITE 200
  AGRICULTURE                                                          PALM SPRINGS 92262
  ENVIRONMENTAL SAFETY &                                              (619) 323-8301
  TOXIC MATERIALS
  LOCAL GOVERNMENT                                                    TOLL FREE
  WATER, PARKS & WILDLIFE                                            1-800-824-5200


                              DAVID G. KELLEY
                    ASSEMBLYMAN, SEVENTY-THIRD DISTRICT
                             RIVERSIDE COUNTY

                               VICE CHAIRMAN
                    WATER, PARKS AND WILDLIFE COMMITTEE

                              February 11, 1988


Ms. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Dear Ms. Hughes:

Thank you for taking the time to come over to my office to
discuss my Statement of Economic Interest Report.  I appreciate
that very much.

As we discussed, I would like to have an F.P.P.C. decision in
writing on what the reporting procedures are when numismatic
coins are used as an investment held by one of my businesses.

Thanks again for all your help.

Best regards,

[signature]
DAVE KELLEY

DK:bjs

M

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 17, 1988

Honorable David G. Kelley
Assemblyman, Seventy-third District
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: 88-073

Dear Assemblyman Kelley:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 16, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
